tickerItems: Madhya Pradesh reorganises State Waqf Board; includes Hindu members for the first time.
PM Modi pays tribute to Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee on his 125th birth anniversary.
Kailash Mansarovar Yatra 2026 begins via upgraded vehicle-based Lipulekh Pass route.
Puri Gajapati Maharaja urges ISKCON to align global Rath Yatra dates with scriptural calendar.
Islamabad to host crucial US-Iran technical negotiations on nuclear program and sanctions relief.
Monsoon dependency and groundwater overuse deepen water stress across major Indian cities.
Russia launches fresh missile and drone strikes on Kyiv ahead of the NATO Summit in Turkey.
Vikram Misri takes charge as India's new Foreign Secretary, ensuring strategic continuity.

topicCards.title: Reorganisation of Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board: Legal and Constitutional Dimensions of Waqf Administration

keyFacts.title: Gazette Notification
keyFacts.desc: The Madhya Pradesh government issued a gazette notification reorganising the 10-member State Waqf Board.

keyFacts.title: New Leadership
keyFacts.desc: Sanwar Patel has been appointed as the Chairman of the reorganised Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board.

keyFacts.title: Inclusive Representation
keyFacts.desc: Manoj Malpani and Animesh Bhargava have been included as Hindu members on the board, marking a significant administrative shift.

keyFacts.title: Regulatory Framework
keyFacts.desc: State Waqf Boards are statutory bodies established under the Waqf Act, 1995, to manage and protect Waqf properties.

staticGkPoints: Waqf Act 1995 — enacted by Parliament to provide for the better administration of Auqaf (properties dedicated as waqf).
Central Waqf Council — established in 1964 under the Ministry of Minority Affairs as an advisory body on waqf administration.

Constitutional Provisions — Article 25 (Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion) and Article 26 (Freedom to manage religious affairs) govern these administrations.
Waqf Tribunal — constituted under Section 83 of the Waqf Act, 1995, to resolve disputes regarding waqf properties.
examinerPerspective: Paper setters frequently target the statutory nature of Waqf Boards; candidates must remember they are established under a Central Act but administered by State Governments.
Common trap: Confusing the Central Waqf Council with a constitutional body. It is strictly a statutory body under the Ministry of Minority Affairs.
State PSCs often ask about the composition and appointing authority of State Waqf Boards (the State Government).
UPSC Mains linkage: GS-2 syllabus topic 'Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies' and issues related to secularism and religious trust administrations.

examRelevance.note: Statutory provisions of the Waqf Act, 1995, and powers of the Central Waqf Council.
Ministry under which Central Waqf Council operates and year of Waqf Act.
Specific appointments and composition changes in State Waqf Boards.

topicCards.title: Analyzing Uttar Pradesh's 'Revenue Surplus' Claims: Fiscal Health vs. Capital Expenditure Realities

keyFacts.title: Revenue Surplus
keyFacts.desc: Uttar Pradesh consistently projects a revenue surplus, which occurs when revenue receipts exceed revenue expenditure.

keyFacts.title: The Structural Issue
keyFacts.desc: Critics point out that the surplus is often achieved by under-spending on crucial social sectors and capital asset creation.

keyFacts.title: Central Dependency
keyFacts.desc: A significant portion of UP's revenue receipts comes from central tax devloution and grants-in-aid rather than its own tax revenue.

keyFacts.title: Capital Expenditure
keyFacts.desc: True economic development requires robust Capital Expenditure (CapEx) which builds long-term productive assets like roads, schools, and hospitals.

staticGkPoints: Revenue Deficit/Surplus — refers to the difference between revenue receipts and revenue expenditures of the government.
FRBM Act 2003 — Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act aims to reduce fiscal deficit and eliminate revenue deficit.
15th Finance Commission — recommended a horizontal devolution formula where Uttar Pradesh received the highest share (17.9%) due to population and distance criteria.
Article 280 — Constitutional provision for the establishment of the Finance Commission of India every five years by the President.
examinerPerspective: Examiners love to test the conceptual difference between Revenue Expenditure (consumptive, non-asset creating) and Capital Expenditure (asset-creating).

A common trap is assuming a 'Revenue Surplus' automatically translates to a healthy overall fiscal deficit; a state can have a revenue surplus but still run a high fiscal deficit due to heavy capital borrowing.
UPSC GS-3 Mains: Direct linkage to 'Government Budgeting' and 'Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it'.
Banking exams frequently ask for definitions of Fiscal Deficit, Revenue Deficit, and Primary Deficit.

examRelevance.note: Conceptual questions on FRBM Act targets, Revenue vs. Capital accounts.
Definitions of fiscal indicators and current recommendations of the Finance Commission.
State-specific budget allocations and fiscal deficit targets under state FRBM acts.

topicCards.title: Deepening Water Stress in India: Monsoon Dependency, Groundwater Depletion, and Reservoir Crisis

keyFacts.title: Urban Rationing
keyFacts.desc: Cities like Mumbai (shrinking lake supply) and Pune (alternate-day water rationing) are facing severe water stress due to delayed or deficient monsoons.

keyFacts.title: Reservoir Depletion
keyFacts.desc: The Central Water Commission (CWC) reports that water levels in India's major reservoirs are significantly below the 10-year average.

keyFacts.title: Groundwater Overuse
keyFacts.desc: India is the largest user of groundwater globally, extracting more than the US and China combined, primarily for agricultural irrigation.

keyFacts.title: Monsoon Dependency
keyFacts.desc: Over 50% of India's cultivable land remains rainfed, making the agricultural economy highly vulnerable to monsoon variability.

staticGkPoints: Central Water Commission (CWC) — premier technical organization in India in the field of water resources, established in 1945.
Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) — constituted under Section 3(3) of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, to regulate groundwater development.
Composite Water Management Index (CWMI) — published by NITI Aayog to assess and improve water resource management across states.
Mihir Shah Committee (2016) — recommended restructuring the CWC and CGWB into a single National Water Commission (NWC).
examinerPerspective: Examiners frequently ask about the statutory origin of the Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) — it is the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, NOT the Water Act, 1974.
UPSC Prelims often tests concepts like 'virtual water export' (water used to grow crops like rice and sugarcane which are then exported).
State PSCs focus on regional micro-irrigation schemes (e.g., Per Drop More Crop under PMKSY).
Mains questions often require integrating climate change, urban planning, and agricultural crop diversification to address water scarcity.

examRelevance.note: Statutory bodies, water conservation schemes, and geographical distribution of rainfall.
Headquarters of CWC, year of Environment Protection Act, and basic water cycle terms.
State-specific river basins, inter-state water disputes, and local water harvesting traditional methods.

topicCards.title: Artificial Nesting Sites ('Bee Hotels'): Innovative Tools for Pollinator Conservation and Urban Biodiversity

keyFacts.title: The Experiment
keyFacts.desc: A scientist constructed 200 'bee hotels' (artificial nesting structures) to study solitary bees and urban pollinator dynamics over three years.

keyFacts.title: Biodiversity Insights
keyFacts.desc: The study revealed unexpected diversity in wild, solitary bees, which do not live in hives but are critical for pollinating wild plants and crops.

keyFacts.title: Parasite Dynamics
keyFacts.desc: The research highlighted how artificial nesting sites can sometimes attract parasites, emphasizing the need for proper design and maintenance.

keyFacts.title: Ecological Value
keyFacts.desc: Pollinators contribute directly to global food security; over 75% of the world's food crops depend at least in part on pollination.

staticGkPoints: Solitary Bees — make up over 90% of the world's 20,000 bee species; unlike honeybees, every female is fertile and builds her own nest.
IPBES Pollination Report — The Intergovernmental Science-Policy Platform on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services highlights that 40% of invertebrate pollinator species face extinction.
World Bee Day — celebrated annually on May 20 to mark the birth of Anton Janša, a pioneer of modern apiculture.
National Bee Board (NBB) — established in India in 2000 to promote scientific beekeeping and support the Sweet Revolution.
examinerPerspective: Examiners target the ecological role of pollinators and the distinction between social bees (honeybees) and solitary bees.
Common trap: Assuming all bees live in hives and produce honey. Solitary bees do neither but are superior pollinators.
UPSC Prelims often asks about symbiotic relationships and ecological services provided by insects.
Mains linkage: GS-3 Environment and Biodiversity conservation strategies beyond traditional national parks.

examRelevance.note: Ecological roles of pollinators, IPBES reports, and conservation initiatives.
Study of bees (Apiculture), World Bee Day date, and National Bee Board.
State-level honey production initiatives and agricultural impact of pollinators.

topicCards.title: US-Iran Technical Negotiations in Islamabad: The Islamabad Memorandum and West Asian Security

keyFacts.title: Neutral Venue
keyFacts.desc: Islamabad, Pakistan is set to host crucial technical negotiations between the United States and Iran around July 11, 2026.

keyFacts.title: The Framework
keyFacts.desc: The talks build upon the 'Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding' signed on June 18, aiming for a comprehensive peace framework.

keyFacts.title: Core Agenda
keyFacts.desc: Key discussion points include Iran's nuclear enrichment levels, sanctions relief, and regional security stabilization.

keyFacts.title: Timeline
keyFacts.desc: Negotiators are working under a strict 60-day timeframe to establish a verifiable compliance mechanism.

staticGkPoints: JCPOA (Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action) — signed in 2015 between Iran and the P5+1 (US, UK, France, Russia, China + Germany).
US Withdrawal — The United States unilaterally withdrew from the JCPOA in 2018 under the Trump administration, reinstating heavy economic sanctions.
IAEA (International Atomic Energy Agency) — established in 1957, headquartered in Vienna, Austria; acts as the global nuclear watchdog.
NPT (Non-Proliferation Treaty) — entered into force in 1970; Iran is a signatory, while India, Pakistan, Israel, and South Sudan are not.
examinerPerspective: Examiners frequently test the composition of the P5+1 group and the status of countries regarding the NPT.
A common trap is confusing the IAEA's headquarters (Vienna) with other UN bodies in Geneva or New York.
UPSC GS-2 Mains: Direct relevance to 'Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests'.

examRelevance.note: JCPOA history, IAEA safeguards, and India's strategic interests in Chabahar Port amidst sanctions.
Impact of Iranian oil sanctions on global crude prices and India's current account deficit.
Geopolitical locations in West Asia (Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf).

topicCards.title: The Pen Pinter Prize: Celebrating Literary Courage and Freedom of Expression

keyFacts.title: The Award
keyFacts.desc: The Pen Pinter Prize is a prestigious literary award given annually to a writer of outstanding literary merit who casts an unflinching gaze on the world.

keyFacts.title: Eligibility
keyFacts.desc: The prize is awarded to a writer who defines themselves as living in the UK, the Republic of Ireland, or the Commonwealth.

keyFacts.title: Indian Connection
keyFacts.desc: Renowned Indian author Arundhati Roy was awarded the Pen Pinter Prize 2024 for her courageous essays and fiction.

keyFacts.title: Companion Award
keyFacts.desc: The winner co-chooses an 'International Writer of Courage' who has been persecuted for speaking out.

staticGkPoints: Established in 2009 — by English PEN in memory of the Nobel Prize-winning British playwright Harold Pinter.
Harold Pinter — won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2005; famous for plays like 'The Birthday Party' and 'The Caretaker'.
English PEN — founded in 1921, it is the founding centre of PEN International, a worldwide writers' association promoting freedom of expression.
First Winner — Tony Harrison was the inaugural recipient of the Pen Pinter Prize in 2009.
examinerPerspective: One-liner exams (SSC, Banking) frequently ask for the names of recent award winners and the country/organization that confers the award.
Common trap: Confusing the Pen Pinter Prize with the Booker Prize. The former focuses heavily on human rights and political courage alongside literary merit.
State PSCs often ask about the works of the winning authors (e.g., Arundhati Roy's 'The God of Small Things' which won the Booker Prize in 1997).

examRelevance.note: Inaugural year, recent winners, and association with Harold Pinter.
General awareness sections covering international cultural and literary awards.
Literary achievements of Indian authors on global platforms.

topicCards.title: 125th Birth Anniversary of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee: Legacy of National Integration and Education

keyFacts.title: The Milestone
keyFacts.desc: July 6, 2026 marks the 125th birth anniversary of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee, born on July 6, 1901, in Calcutta.

keyFacts.title: Political Legacy
keyFacts.desc: He founded the Bharatiya Jana Sangh in 1951, which was the ideological predecessor of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

keyFacts.title: Academic Feat
keyFacts.desc: He became the youngest Vice-Chancellor of the University of Calcutta in 1934 at the age of 33.

keyFacts.title: Nationalist Stance
keyFacts.desc: He strongly opposed the special status of Jammu and Kashmir under Article 370, coining the slogan: 'Ek desh mein do vidhan, do pradhan aur do nishan nahi chalenge'.

staticGkPoints: First Cabinet — Served as the Minister for Industry and Supply in Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru's interim cabinet.
Resignation (1950) — Resigned from the cabinet in protest against the Liaquat-Nehru Pact, which he felt failed to protect minorities in East Pakistan.
All India Hindu Mahasabha — Served as its President from 1943 to 1944.
Demise — Passed away under detention in Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir, on June 23, 1953.
examinerPerspective: History and Polity sections frequently feature Dr. Mookerjee's contributions to post-independence consolidation.
Common trap: Believing he was a member of the Congress party throughout his career. He was an independent in the first cabinet and later founded the Jana Sangh.
Paper setters often ask about the specific portfolio he held (Industry and Supply) and the reason for his cabinet resignation (Liaquat-Nehru Pact).
UPSC Mains linkage: GS-1 Modern Indian History and Post-independence consolidation.

examRelevance.note: Post-independence consolidation, interim cabinet composition, and ideological movements.
Founding year of Bharatiya Jana Sangh (1951) and his ministerial portfolio.
Role in Bengal politics, partition debates, and educational reforms.

topicCards.title: FIDE World Chess Championship and India's Rising Dominance in Global Chess

keyFacts.title: Youngest Challenger
keyFacts.desc: India's D. Gukesh made history as the youngest-ever challenger for the FIDE World Chess Championship.

keyFacts.title: Global Governing Body
keyFacts.desc: FIDE (Fédération Internationale des Échecs) regulates all international chess competitions and awards player titles.

keyFacts.title: Grandmaster Boom
keyFacts.desc: India has crossed over 85 Grandmasters, reflecting a massive structural growth in the country's chess ecosystem.

keyFacts.title: Highest Honour
keyFacts.desc: The Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna is India's highest sporting honour, frequently awarded to top chess achievers.

staticGkPoints: FIDE Founded — July 20, 1924, in Paris, France; its headquarters is currently located in Lausanne, Switzerland.
First Indian GM — Viswanathan Anand became India's first Grandmaster in 1988 and went on to win the World Championship five times.
First Khel Ratna — The inaugural Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna (now Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna) was awarded to Viswanathan Anand in 1991-92.
First Female GM — Koneru Humpy became India's first female Grandmaster in 2002.
examinerPerspective: Sports GK is highly factual. Questions often target 'firsts' in Indian sports history.
Common trap: Confusing the headquarters of FIDE (Lausanne) with other sports bodies like FIFA (Zurich) or ICC (Dubai).
SSC and Banking exams frequently ask about the youngest Indian Grandmasters and recent tournament venues.

examRelevance.note: First Grandmaster of India, FIDE headquarters, and recent sports awards.
Current affairs questions on sports personalities and international championships.
Regional sports achievements and state-sponsored chess academies.

topicCards.title: The Rath Yatra Dispute: Scriptural Traditions of Puri Jagannath Temple vs. ISKCON's Global Celebrations

keyFacts.title: The Core Issue
keyFacts.desc: Puri Gajapati Maharaja Dibyasingha Deb has urged ISKCON to stop organizing Rath Yatras on dates other than those prescribed by Hindu scriptures.

keyFacts.title: Scriptural Date
keyFacts.desc: According to the Skanda Purana, the Rath Yatra must strictly begin on the 'Dwitiya Tithi' (second day) of the 'Ashadha Shukla Paksha' (waxing phase of moon in June/July).

keyFacts.title: ISKCON's Stand
keyFacts.desc: The International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON) often hosts Rath Yatras on different dates abroad due to local permissions and weekend logistics.

keyFacts.title: Gajapati's Role
keyFacts.desc: The Gajapati Maharaja of Puri is the traditional king and the 'Adhyasevaka' (first servant) of Lord Jagannath, holding supreme authority over temple rituals.

staticGkPoints: Jagannath Temple Architecture — Built in the 12th century AD by King Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty.
Kalinga Style — The temple is a classic example of Kalinga architecture, consisting of the Deula (sanctum), Jagamohana (assembly hall), Natamandira (festival hall), and Bhogamandapa (offering hall).
White Pagoda — Historically, European sailors referred to the Puri Jagannath Temple as the 'White Pagoda', while the Konark Sun Temple was called the 'Black Pagoda'.

Nabakalebara Festival — A ritual associated with the temple where the wooden idols of Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra are replaced with new ones made of neem wood, occurring every 8, 12, or 19 years.
examinerPerspective: Art and Culture sections frequently test temple architecture styles (Nagara, Dravida, Vesara, Kalinga).
Common trap: Confusing the builder of the Jagannath Temple (Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva) with the builder of the Konark Sun Temple (Narasimhadeva I).
UPSC Prelims often asks about traditional festivals, their lunar calendar dates, and associated dynasties.
Mains linkage: GS-1 Indian Culture - Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

examRelevance.note: Kalinga architecture features, Eastern Ganga Dynasty history, and Nabakalebara ritual details.
Builder of the temple, 'White Pagoda' terminology, and state of origin.
Odishan temple architecture and regional cultural festivals.

topicCards.title: Appointment of Vikram Misri as India's Foreign Secretary: Strategic Continuity in Indian Diplomacy

keyFacts.title: The Appointment
keyFacts.desc: Senior diplomat Vikram Misri took charge as India's Foreign Secretary, succeeding Vinay Mohan Kwatra.

keyFacts.title: China Expertise
keyFacts.desc: Misri, a 1989-batch IFS officer, served as India's Ambassador to China during the crucial Galwan Valley clash period.

keyFacts.title: National Security
keyFacts.desc: Prior to this appointment, he served as the Deputy National Security Advisor (NSA) of India.

keyFacts.title: Role & Duties
keyFacts.desc: The Foreign Secretary is the administrative head of the Ministry of External Affairs and principal advisor to the External Affairs Minister.

staticGkPoints: Indian Foreign Service (IFS) — established in 1946 under the recommendation of the Federal Public Service Commission.
First Foreign Secretary — K. P. S. Menon served as the first Foreign Secretary of independent India (1948–1952).
First Female Foreign Secretary — Chokila Iyer became India's first female Foreign Secretary in 2001.
Constitutional Status — The civil services of India, including IFS, are governed by Article 312 (All India Services) and Article 311 of the Constitution.
examinerPerspective: Appointments of key secretaries (Foreign, Home, Finance, Cabinet) are high-yield areas for SSC and Banking exams.
Common trap: Believing the Foreign Service is a separate constitutional body. It is a Central Civil Service (Group A) under the executive control of the MEA.
Examiners often ask about the predecessor of newly appointed high-ranking officials.

examRelevance.note: Name of the current Foreign Secretary, first female Foreign Secretary, and parent ministry.
Key administrative appointments in the Government of India.
Structure of the Ministry of External Affairs and civil service classifications.

topicCards.title: 'The Golden Road: How Ancient India Transformed the World' by William Dalrymple

keyFacts.title: The Book
keyFacts.desc: 'The Golden Road' explores India's profound cultural, mathematical, and religious influence across Asia and the world from 250 BC to 1200 AD.

keyFacts.title: The Author
keyFacts.desc: William Dalrymple is an acclaimed Scottish historian, art historian, and curator, known for his narrative history books on India.

keyFacts.title: Key Theme
keyFacts.desc: The book challenges the 'Silk Road' narrative by highlighting the 'Golden Road' of Indian maritime and overland trade that exported Buddhism, Hinduism, and mathematics.

keyFacts.title: Cultural Export
keyFacts.desc: It details how Indian concepts like zero, Sanskrit literature, and temple architecture shaped Southeast Asian empires like Angkor Wat and Srivijaya.

staticGkPoints: Jaipur Literature Festival — William Dalrymple is the co-founder and co-director of the festival, established in 2006.
Notable Works — Author of 'The Anarchy: The Relentless Rise of the East India Company', 'White Mughals', and 'The Last Mughal'.
Duff Cooper Prize — Dalrymple won this prestigious British literary award in 1997 for his book 'From the Holy Mountain'.
Sahitya Akademi Translation Prize — His works have been widely translated and recognized by literary bodies globally.
examinerPerspective: Books and Authors sections in competitive exams frequently feature prominent historians writing on Indian history.
Common trap: Confusing William Dalrymple's books with those of other popular historians like Ramachandra Guha or Manu S. Pillai.
Questions often ask for the specific historical period covered in the book (Ancient India, 250 BC to 1200 AD).

examRelevance.note: Author of 'The Golden Road' and other famous historical books.
Current books and authors in the general awareness section.
Historical themes of cultural export to Southeast Asia (Suvarnabhumi).

topicCards.title: Kailash Mansarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass: Geopolitical, Cultural, and Infrastructure Significance

keyFacts.title: The Pilgrimage
keyFacts.desc: The annual Kailash Mansarovar Yatra has commenced, with the first batch of pilgrims flagged off by Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami.

keyFacts.title: Infrastructure Upgrade
keyFacts.desc: The route through the Lipulekh Pass has been upgraded to be largely vehicle-based, significantly reducing trekking time and physical strain.

keyFacts.title: Alternative Routes
keyFacts.desc: Historically, the Yatra is conducted through two main routes managed by the Ministry of External Affairs: Lipulekh Pass (Uttarakhand) and Nathu La Pass (Sikkim).

keyFacts.title: Border Development
keyFacts.desc: The motorable road benefits remote border communities in the Vyas Valley and strengthens India's border infrastructure.

staticGkPoints: Lipulekh Pass — a Himalayan pass situated at a tri-junction between India (Uttarakhand), China (Tibet), and Nepal.
Mount Kailash — standing at 6,638 meters, it is considered sacred in four religions: Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Bön.
Lake Mansarovar — the world's highest freshwater lake, situated at 4,590 meters above sea level, feeding major rivers like the Indus, Sutlej, Brahmaputra, and Karnali (Ghara).
Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam (KMVN) — the nodal agency responsible for logistics on the Indian side of the Lipulekh route.
examinerPerspective: Geography and Heritage sections frequently target Himalayan passes and trans-boundary river origins.
Common trap: Confusing the origin of the Brahmaputra (near Mansarovar/Angsi Glacier) with the Ganga (Gangotri Glacier in India).
The geopolitical dispute over the Lipulekh Pass (Kalapani territory dispute between India and Nepal) is a high-yield area for UPSC Mains GS-2.
State PSCs (especially Uttarakhand PSC) ask detailed questions about the valleys and rivers along the Yatra route (e.g., Kali River, Vyas Valley).

examRelevance.note: Himalayan geography, trans-boundary rivers, and India-Nepal border relations.
Location of Lipulekh Pass, Nathu La Pass, and rivers originating from Mansarovar.
Uttarakhand and Sikkim geography, local administrative bodies involved in the Yatra.

mcqData.q: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee, whose 125th birth anniversary was observed on July 6, 2026, served as the Minister of which portfolio in Jawaharlal Nehru's interim cabinet?
mcqData.explanation: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee served as the Minister for Industry and Supply in Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru's interim cabinet. He resigned from the cabinet in 1950 in protest against the Liaquat-Nehru Pact.

mcqData.q: Who was the first recipient of the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award (now Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award), established in 1991-92?
mcqData.explanation: Viswanathan Anand, India's first Chess Grandmaster (1988), was the inaugural recipient of the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award in 1991-92 for his outstanding achievements in chess.

mcqData.q: The famous Jagannath Temple of Puri, recently in the news regarding the Rath Yatra dispute, was constructed in the 12th century by which ruler of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty?
mcqData.explanation: The Jagannath Temple of Puri was built in the 12th century AD by King Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty. Narasimhadeva I built the Konark Sun Temple.

mcqData.q: Who was the first Foreign Secretary of independent India, serving from 1948 to 1952?
mcqData.explanation: K. P. S. Menon (Kumara Padmanabha Sivasankara Menon) served as the first Foreign Secretary of independent India from 1948 to 1952. Chokila Iyer was the first female Foreign Secretary (2001).

mcqData.q: The book 'The Golden Road: How Ancient India Transformed the World', which highlights India's historical cultural export, is authored by which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: 'The Golden Road' is authored by the acclaimed historian William Dalrymple. The book details India's cultural, mathematical, and religious influence across Asia from 250 BC to 1200 AD.

mcqData.q: The Lipulekh Pass, a key route for the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra, forms a tri-junction between which of the following three regions/countries?
mcqData.explanation: Lipulekh Pass is a Himalayan pass situated at a tri-junction between India (Uttarakhand), China (Tibet), and Nepal. It is a key route for trade and the Kailash Mansarovar pilgrimage.

mcqData.q: Under which Ministry of the Government of India does the Central Waqf Council, established in 1964, function as an advisory body?
mcqData.explanation: The Central Waqf Council is a statutory body established in 1964 by the Government of India under the Waqf Act, and it functions under the administrative control of the Ministry of Minority Affairs.

mcqData.q: Under the 15th Finance Commission's horizontal devolution formula, which Indian state has been allocated the highest share of central taxes?
mcqData.explanation: Uttar Pradesh has been allocated the highest share of central taxes (17.9%) under the 15th Finance Commission's horizontal devolution formula, primarily due to its large population and distance criteria.

mcqData.q: World Bee Day is celebrated globally on May 20 to commemorate the birth anniversary of Anton Janša, a pioneer of modern apiculture. Which international organization officially declared this day?
mcqData.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly, on the recommendation of the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), declared May 20 as World Bee Day in December 2017 to raise awareness about the importance of pollinators.

mcqData.q: The Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) of India was constituted under which of the following legislations?
opts: Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
mcqData.explanation: The Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) was constituted under Section 3(3) of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, to regulate and control groundwater development and management in the country.

mcqData.q: The prestigious Pen Pinter Prize, established in 2009, is awarded annually by English PEN in memory of which Nobel Prize-winning playwright?
mcqData.explanation: The Pen Pinter Prize was established in 2009 by English PEN in memory of the Nobel Prize-winning British playwright Harold Pinter, who was a passionate advocate for human rights.

mcqData.q: In July 2026, Russia launched fresh attacks on Kyiv ahead of a key NATO summit. In which country is the July 2026 NATO summit scheduled to be hosted?
mcqData.explanation: As reported in the news, the fresh Russian missile and drone attacks on Kyiv occurred ahead of the scheduled NATO summit in Turkey.

mainsQuestions.q: Examine the statutory and administrative framework governing Waqf properties in India. In light of recent administrative reforms, discuss the challenges of ensuring transparency, inclusivity, and secular principles in the management of religious trusts.
mainsQuestions.hint: Key angles: Waqf Act 1995 provisions → Role of State Waqf Boards and Central Waqf Council → Inclusion of non-minority members and its legal validity under Articles 25 & 26 → Balancing religious autonomy with state regulation for public interest.

mainsQuestions.q: A 'Revenue Surplus' budget is often projected as a sign of robust fiscal health. However, analyze how a revenue surplus can sometimes mask structural deficiencies in a state's developmental spending, particularly with respect to capital asset creation.
mainsQuestions.hint: Key angles: Definition of Revenue Surplus vs. Fiscal Deficit → How under-spending on social sectors (health, education) artificially inflates surplus → Importance of Capital Expenditure (CapEx) for long-term GDP growth → Recommendations of the Finance Commission on fiscal consolidation.

mainsQuestions.q: With over half of India's agriculture remaining rainfed, monsoon variability poses a severe threat to water and food security. Discuss the geographical factors contributing to India's deepening water stress and suggest sustainable groundwater management strategies.
mainsQuestions.hint: Key angles: Spatial and temporal distribution of Indian Monsoon → Over-exploitation of groundwater (sugarcane/rice cultivation nexus) → Role of Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) → Solutions: Micro-irrigation, rainwater harvesting, crop diversification, and community-led water budgeting.

summaryTable.data: Sanwar Patel appointed Chairman; Hindu members included for the first time.
Analysis shows surplus is driven by low capital spending and high central transfers.
Monsoon dependency and groundwater depletion lead to urban water rationing.
Artificial nests reveal high diversity of solitary bees, crucial for urban pollination.
Islamabad to host technical talks on nuclear program and sanctions relief on July 11.
Literary award by English PEN; Arundhati Roy won the 2024 edition.
125th birth anniversary observed; founder of Jana Sangh and first Industry Minister.
D. Gukesh is the youngest challenger; FIDE HQ is in Lausanne, Switzerland.
Puri Gajapati urges ISKCON to align global Rath Yatra with Ashadha Shukla Dwitiya.
Appointed as India's new Foreign Secretary, succeeding Vinay Mohan Kwatra.
New book by William Dalrymple on India's ancient cultural and mathematical export.
